stories.name: link-model-expiry-migration
stories.title: Link model, expiry & migration
stories.description: Extend the InviteLink record with expiresAt/usageCount/expiryNotified/expiryAcknowledged, add the isExpired predicate (with the createdAt+DAY_MS fallback for legacy records), the usage/notification-flag storage helpers, set expiry fields at creation, and add the one-shot idempotent migrateInviteLinks() that backfills legacy records and resolves the retired muted flag.
stories.notes: Foundation story — every other story (S2-S5) reads isExpired/incrementInviteLinkUsage/markInviteLinkExpiryNotified/markInviteLinkExpiryAcknowledged/migrateInviteLinks from this module's exports (architecture.md Seams: 'inviteLinkStorage ↔ everything'). Lands first so downstream stories consume the real implementation rather than a mock (Story Design Principle 1 exception is unneeded here because implementation is sequential). AC-MIGRATE-6 (idempotency) and the migration-order aspects of AC-MIGRATE-3/4/5 are unit-tested here at the single-invocation level; the concurrent/interleaved version of migration idempotency is AC-INV-6, owned by the S5 integration story.

stories.name: join-flow-expiry-usage-enforcement
stories.title: Expiry & usage enforcement in the join flow
stories.description: Drop incoming join requests referencing an expired invite link at the existing mute gate in handleJoinRequest, and increment usageCount in approveJoinRequest only after inviteByNpub resolves {ok:true} — approval itself stays ungated on link liveness.

excludes: app/src/lib/marmot/inviteLinkStorage.ts (owned by S1; consume isExpired/incrementInviteLinkUsage, do not reimplement)
app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx leaveGroup wiring (~line 1581-1613, owned by S4 for the AC-DEEPLINK-4 clear call)

stories.notes: MarmotContext.tsx is also touched by S4, at a different, non-overlapping call site (leaveGroup wiring vs. approveJoinRequest) — flagged as a shared surface, same caution as i18n.ts between S3/S4. Depends on S1 for isExpired and incrementInviteLinkUsage.

stories.name: manage-overlay-redesign
stories.title: Manage overlay redesign
stories.description: Redesign each ManageInviteLinksModal row to show created/expiry time-of-day-precision (or relative) copy and a usage-count string, replace the mute Switch with a confirmed trashcan hard-delete, apply distinct expired-row styling that live-updates while the modal is open, and render a translated empty state when a group has no links.

includes: app/src/lib/i18n.ts (S3's keys only: relative-time strings, usage-count copy, expired marker, empty-state, trashcan confirmation)
excludes: app/src/lib/i18n.ts bell-row copy keys (owned by S4)
app/src/lib/marmot/inviteLinkStorage.ts (owned by S1; consume isExpired/deleteInviteLink/loadInviteLinks, do not reimplement expiry logic)

stories.notes: i18n.ts is a shared surface with S4 — each story adds only the Copy keys it introduces (S3: row/empty-state/confirmation copy; S4: bell-row copy). AC-UI-8's live-updating expired treatment may read the same tick source S4's sweep uses, but must not import inviteExpirySweep.ts directly (manageOverlay owns no notifications dependency per architecture.md's module map) — use a local interval/rerender, consistent with 'renders inviteLinkStorage state' as manageOverlay's only owned data (none).

stories.name: expiry-notification-deep-link
stories.title: Expiry notification & deep-link
stories.description: Add the inviteExpiries unread-bell slice (derived on init from persisted link flags, mirroring the joinRequests slice), the client-side expiry sweep with a module-level in-flight latch, a fourth NotificationBell render block linking to the manageLinks deep-link, the /groups manageLinks=1 query-param handling that opens the overlay once the detail view has rendered, and wiring clearInviteExpiries into the existing group-leave/abandon purge alongside clearInviteLinksForGroup.

includes: app/src/lib/marmot/leaveGroupImpl.ts (add clearInviteExpiries to the unconditional purge sequence, AC-DEEPLINK-4)
app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx (leaveGroup wiring, ~line 1581-1613: thread clearInviteExpiries into the Deps object)
excludes: app/src/lib/i18n.ts row/empty-state/confirmation copy keys (owned by S3)
app/src/lib/marmot/inviteLinkStorage.ts (owned by S1; consume its exported helpers, do not reimplement)

stories.notes: AC-LOCALE-1 is a cross-cutting completeness AC (spanned_by S3+S4 in the top-level cross_cutting_acs array) assigned here as its primary owning story because S4 lands last among the copy-introducing stories, giving Mode 3 a natural single Part-B verification point over the full new-key set. inviteExpirySweep.ts is a new file (Design Decision 8/11, architecture.md's Order-Sensitive Composition flow 'expiry notification'); the single-invocation/basic-notify-once behavior is unit-tested here, while the StrictMode/overlapping-interval concurrent-interleaving property (AC-INV-1/2/4) is owned by the S5 integration story against this story's real, already-landed implementation. MarmotContext.tsx leaveGroup wiring is a shared surface with S2 at a distinct call site.

stories.name: invite-link-lifecycle-order-invariants
stories.title: Cross-module order-sensitive invariant verification
stories.description: Integration-verify the six whole-flow, order/concurrency-sensitive guarantees architecture.md's Order-Sensitive Composition section calls out across inviteLinkStorage, joinFlow, and notifications, against the real, already-implemented producers from S1/S2/S4: exactly-once sweep notification under concurrent/rapid sweep invocations (AC-INV-1), reload-safe notification derivation from persisted flags (AC-INV-2), correct expiryNotified stamping across arbitrary link mixes during migration (AC-INV-3), crash-safe stamp-before-bump ordering under interrupted sweep-then-notify (AC-INV-4), lost-update-free usageCount increments under concurrent approveJoinRequest calls on the same nonce (AC-INV-5), and idempotent/non-racing migrateInviteLinks under repeated or concurrent invocation (AC-INV-6).

excludes: Any production source file under app/src/ — this story verifies the assembled whole, it does not re-implement inviteLinkStorage/joinFlow/notifications logic (Module Ownership principle 5 exemption is for reaching across modules to verify, not to duplicate their logic)

stories.notes: FR-A7 integration story, triggered by architecture.md's '## Order-Sensitive Composition' signal and the six 'Spans modules:' lines Mode 1 wrote under acceptance-criteria.md's Cross-Cutting Invariants. Depends on S1 (inviteLinkStorage), S2 (joinFlow), and S4 (notifications) — the union of the six ACs' own Spans-modules sets — so story_order places it last. Runs against real producers per the Mock Strategy integration-story exception: never mock inviteLinkStorage/joinRequestHandler/approveJoinRequest/inviteExpirySweep, and no mocks-registry.json entry is added for them.

dependency_notes: S1 is the foundation: it owns the InviteLink record shape and the isExpired/incrementInviteLinkUsage/markInviteLinkExpiryNotified/markInviteLinkExpiryAcknowledged/migrateInviteLinks exports every other story consumes (architecture.md Seams). S2 and S3 both depend only on S1 and are mutually independent (join-flow enforcement vs. manage-overlay rendering touch disjoint files), but are sequenced S2-before-S3 as the lower-complexity story first per Story Design Principle 4. S4 depends on S1 for the link-flag fields it derives inviteExpiries from, and touches a leaveGroupImpl.ts/MarmotContext.tsx call site adjacent to (but non-overlapping with) S2's. S5, the FR-A7 integration story, depends on S1+S2+S4 (the union of its six owned cross-cutting ACs' participating modules) and runs last, verifying real, already-landed producers rather than mocks.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks are introduced by this epic — stories implement sequentially (Module Ownership principle 5: parallel story implementation is a non-goal) and each downstream story consumes S1's real, already-landed storage helpers directly. mocks-registry.json is seeded empty with all_resolved: true and requires no further action.
